generated_oml:
// C3: Physical sensing components
instance ManufacturingSensorTraceCollector : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Collects contextual data traces from physical manufacturing systems including sensor readings and operational logs for Digital Shadow integration."
    DTDFVocab:producedData ManufacturingSensorDataTransmitted
]

instance OperationalLogAggregator : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Aggregates operational logs and event traces from manufacturing equipment to support Digital Shadow data collection."
    DTDFVocab:producedData OperationalLogDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance ManufacturingSensorDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ManufacturingSensorTraceCollector
    base:desc "Transmits raw sensor data traces from physical manufacturing systems to the Digital Shadow for passive ingestion."
]

instance OperationalLogDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OperationalLogAggregator
    base:desc "Transmits aggregated operational logs and event traces from manufacturing equipment to the Digital Shadow."
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance SysMLMultiLayeredSystemModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML-based multi-layered system model including Internal Block Diagrams (IBDs) and state machines for requirements, functions, logics, and physics layers."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLIntegrationEnabler, ModelTransformationPipeline
]

instance ACMEComponentConnectorModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "ACME Architecture Description Language model defining component/connector-based system decomposition for manufacturing systems."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ADLIntegrationEnabler, ModelTransformationPipeline
]

instance AADLComponentBasedModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "AADL model representing component-based architecture with spatial representations for manufacturing equipment."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ADLIntegrationEnabler, ModelTransformationPipeline
]

instance MontiArcAutomatonModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MontiArc Automaton model for behavioral modeling of manufacturing system components with state machine capabilities."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MontiArcIntegrationEnabler, ModelTransformationPipeline
]

instance SimulinkBehavioralSimulationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink model for detailed behavioral simulation and dynamic system analysis of manufacturing processes."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulationIntegrationEnabler
]

instance CppControlAlgorithmModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Custom control algorithms implemented in C++ for manufacturing system optimization and real-time control."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CustomLogicIntegrationEnabler
]

instance JavaDataProcessingModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Java-based data processing components for interpreting and transforming Digital Shadow data into actionable insights."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo CustomLogicIntegrationEnabler
]

instance ManufacturingContextualData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated contextual data from physical manufacturing systems including sensor traces and operational logs."
    DTDFVocab:fromData ManufacturingSensorDataTransmitted, OperationalLogDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTransformationEngine, DigitalShadowIntegrationEnabler
]

instance DigitalShadowDataStructure : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Structured Digital Shadow data representation derived from raw manufacturing system data traces."
    DTDFVocab:fromData ManufacturingContextualData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DigitalShadowAnalysisEnabler, FeedbackLoopController
]

// C11: Enablers
instance SysMLIntegrationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Tool interoperability framework enabling SysML model integration with other ADLs and simulation tools."
    DTDFVocab:enables SysMLModelingService, MultiPerspectiveVisualizationService
]

instance ADLIntegrationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Integration framework for ACME, AADL, and MontiArc models enabling component/connector-based system analysis."
    DTDFVocab:enables ComponentBasedAnalysisService, BehavioralModelingService
]

instance MontiArcIntegrationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MontiArc-specific integration enabling state machine and automaton-based behavioral modeling."
    DTDFVocab:enables BehavioralModelingService, StateMonitoringService
]

instance SimulationIntegrationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Integration of Simulink behavioral models with other system models for comprehensive simulation analysis."
    DTDFVocab:enables DynamicSimulationService, ControlAlgorithmInsight
]

instance CustomLogicIntegrationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Framework for integrating C++ and Java custom logic components with Digital Twin models."
    DTDFVocab:enables CustomControlService, DataProcessingService
]

instance ModelTransformationPipeline : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Model transformation pipeline enabling interoperability between SysML, ADLs, and simulation tools."
    DTDFVocab:enables ModelConsistencyService
]

instance DataTransformationEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Software component enabling passive-to-active data transformation from raw sensor traces to structured Digital Shadow data."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataAbstractionService
]

instance DigitalShadowIntegrationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Integration framework for Digital Shadow data structures with active Digital Twin services."
    DTDFVocab:enables ShadowToTwinService
]

instance DigitalShadowAnalysisEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Analysis tools for interpreting Digital Shadow data to derive system state insights."
    DTDFVocab:enables StateMonitoringService, AnomalyDetectionService
]

instance FeedbackLoopController : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Controller implementing feedback loops between Digital Twin analysis and physical system actuation via ADL-defined interfaces."
    DTDFVocab:enables ClosedLoopControlService
]

// C6: Services
instance SysMLModelingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing multi-layered system modeling using SysML including IBDs and state machines."
    DTDFVocab:provides SystemArchitectureInsight, ModelValidationInsight
]

]

]

]

]

instance MultiPerspectiveVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing visualization of multi-layered system models for heterogeneous stakeholders."

]

// C1: Acting Components
instance ManufacturingActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Physical actuator component receiving control signals from Digital Twin services to adjust manufacturing system parameters."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The **Digital Twin (DT) constellation** in the document is implicitly defined through the **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** framework, which orchestrates the system via **multi-layered abstraction models** (requirements, functional architecture, logical architecture, and product/technical layers). The **architecture patterns** are derived from **architecture description languages (ADLs)** like ACME, AADL, or MontiArc, which describe **component integration** through **component & connector perspectives**, including interfaces, interconnections, and composition. **Resource management** is not explicitly detailed, but the use of **simulation tools (e.g., MathWorks Simulink, C++/Java code)** and **behavioral modeling (state machines, automata)** suggests a **hybrid orchestration** combining formal and executable models. **System-wide coordination** is enabled by integrating heterogeneous tools and stakeholders, though no specific orchestration protocol (e.g., event-driven, service-oriented) is mentioned. The document does not address **real-time constraints** or **dynamic reconfiguration** mechanisms."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system emphasizes **model fidelity** through **multi-layered abstraction** (requirements → functional → logical → physical), validated via **MBSE methodologies** (e.g., **RFLP, OOSEM, SPES**). **Validation and verification (V&V)** methods include:
  - **Structural validation**: Ensuring component-connector consistency via **ADLs (e.g., AADL, ACME)** and **SysML IBDs**.
  - **Behavioral validation**: Cross-checking **state machines (SysML v1.7)** and **simulation outputs (Simulink)** against physical system responses.
  - **Uncertainty quantification**: Implicitly addressed through **data aggregation and abstraction** in Digital Shadows, where **contextual traces** are refined to reduce noise before DT processing.
  **Error detection/correction** relies on **active DT components** interpreting discrepancies between digital and physical states, though specific mechanisms (e.g., **Kalman filters, machine learning**) are not detailed. **Quality assurance** spans **stakeholder integration** (heterogeneous perspectives) and **tool interoperability** (e.g., SysML-Simulink co-simulation)."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The document highlights **horizontal integration** through the **interoperability requirements** of **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)**, which necessitates **data exchange between heterogeneous tools and modeling notations** (e.g., SysML, ADLs, Simulink, C++/Java). **Interoperability standards** are implied via the use of **architecture description languages (ADLs)** like AADL or MontiArc, which standardize component interfaces and connections. **Dependencies on external systems** are managed through **integration patterns** such as **component & connector perspectives**, where external systems (e.g., enterprise systems, other DTs) are treated as **connected components** with defined interfaces. The document does not specify **data exchange protocols** (e.g., OPC UA, MQTT) or **enterprise integration standards** (e.g., SOA, microservices), but the emphasis on **heterogeneous stakeholder integration** suggests a need for **standardized APIs or middleware** to bridge disparate tools. No explicit mention of **Digital Twin-to-Digital Twin (DT-to-DT) integration** is provided."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system adheres to **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE) standards**, particularly leveraging **SysML (Systems Modeling Language)** for system modeling across abstraction layers. **Architecture Description Languages (ADLs)** such as **ACME**, **AADL**, **MontiArc**, and **SysML IBDs** provide standardized component-connector-based system descriptions. Behavioral modeling aligns with **SysML state machines (v1.7)** and **MontiArc Automaton**, ensuring compliance with formalized system behavior specifications. Interoperability is facilitated through integration with **MathWorks Simulink** and other simulation tools, adhering to broader **modeling and simulation standards** (e.g., [29], [30]). While no explicit industry-specific standards (e.g., ISO, IEC) or certification frameworks are mentioned, the use of **MBSE methodologies** and **ADLs** implies adherence to **systems engineering best practices** and **interoperability protocols** for heterogeneous toolchains. The Digital Shadow Reference Model (Fig. 1) suggests alignment with **UML-based class modeling standards** for data trace aggregation."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system, as described in the context documents, integrates **cybersecurity protocols** aligned with **Industrie 4.0 standards**, particularly through the **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)** framework (referenced in [46], [47], and [50]). The AAS provides a standardized **access control mechanism** via **submodels** (e.g., *Security*, *Communication*) that enforce **role-based authentication** and **encryption** for data exchange. **Threat mitigation strategies** are implied through the **RAMI4.0 reference architecture** ([48], [49]), which mandates **segmentation of network layers** (e.g., Field Level, Station Level) to isolate critical components. **Fail-safe mechanisms** are supported by **Functional Mockup Interface (FMI)** ([51]) for simulation model exchange, ensuring deterministic behavior in safety-critical scenarios. **Risk assessment** is implicitly addressed via **conformance relations** in reference models ([41]), which validate structural integrity and compliance with **IEC 62443** (cybersecurity for industrial automation systems). The **MQTT protocol** ([52]), leveraged for lightweight IoT communication, includes **TLS/SSL encryption** for secure data transmission. **Open-source IoT platforms** like **ThingsBoard** ([53]) further enable **device authentication** via **JWT/OAuth2** and **data anonymization** through configurable **attribute masking** in telemetry streams. No explicit mention of **consent management** or **data sharing agreements** is provided in the documents."
]
